meaning: I AM WHO I AM; the eternal, self-existent, covenant name of God revealed to Moses
God; the Creator; plural of majesty emphasizing the fullness and power of God
My Lord; emphasizes God's absolute sovereignty and ownership over all creation
Shortened form of Yahweh; used in praise expressions (Hallelu-Yah = Praise the LORD)
God; the Mighty One; the strong and powerful deity
God; singular poetic form emphasizing the uniqueness and majesty of God
God Almighty; the all-sufficient, all-powerful God who is more than enough
God Most High; the supreme deity exalted above all other powers and beings
The God Who Sees Me; given by Hagar when God saw her in her distress
The Eternal God; the God of eternity whose purposes and nature never change
Mighty God; the powerful, warrior God; a title of the Messiah in Isaiah's prophecy
God, the God of Israel; emphasizing the covenant relationship between God and His people
God of Truth; the faithful God whose word is completely trustworthy and reliable
Gracious God; the merciful deity who shows undeserved favor
The LORD Will Provide; named by Abraham when God provided a ram at the place of sacrifice
The LORD is My Banner; named by Moses after victory over Amalek, God is our rallying point
The LORD is Peace; named by Gideon after God granted peace in a time of fear
The LORD Who Heals; God's declaration that He is the divine physician for body and soul
The LORD Our Righteousness; the Messianic name declaring God provides the righteousness we need
The LORD is There; the name for the restored city of Jerusalem, God's abiding presence
The LORD of Hosts / LORD Almighty; commander of the heavenly armies and all creation
The LORD Who Sanctifies You; God sets apart and consecrates His people for Himself
The LORD is My Shepherd; God leads, feeds, protects, and guides His flock
The LORD is My Light; God dispels darkness and illuminates our path
The LORD is My Strength; God empowers those who trust in Him
The Almighty; the all-sufficient God who is more than enough for every need
Most High; the God who is exalted above all other gods and beings
Lord, Master; the sovereign ruler to whom everything belongs
Jealous God; God's passionate commitment to exclusive relationship with His people
The Rock; God's unchanging, stable, and reliable nature as our foundation
Holy One of Israel; God's absolute moral perfection and otherness from creation
Redeemer; the kinsman-redeemer who buys back what was lost and restores covenant relationship
Judge; God's righteous and impartial administration of justice for all people
King; God's sovereign reign over all creation, nations, and history
Our Father; God's tender, parental relationship with His covenant people
The Living God; God who is alive, active, and the source of all life
Shield; God's protective covering over those who take refuge in Him
High Fortress / Stronghold; God as a high, inaccessible place of safety and refuge
Rock of Israel; God as the immovable foundation and protector of the nation Israel
Lord; the Greek equivalent of Yahweh/Adonai; supreme authority and ownership
God; the supreme divine being; used for the Father, and remarkably for Jesus (John 1:1)
Almighty / All-Ruling; the God who holds all power and rules over everything
Savior; the one who delivers from sin, death, and judgment
Christ / Anointed One; the Greek equivalent of Messiah; the one anointed by God for redemption
The Word; the eternal divine reason and expression of God incarnated in Jesus Christ
God with Us; the Messianic name fulfilled in the incarnation of Jesus Christ
Good Shepherd; the one who lays down His life for the sheep
Lamb; the sacrificial lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world
High Priest; the perfect and eternal high priest who intercedes on behalf of humanity
Advocate / Comforter / Helper; title of the Holy Spirit and of Jesus as our intercessor
Holy Spirit; the third person of the Trinity, the Spirit of God who indwells believers
The Holy One; perfectly set apart from sin; used of Jesus as the one consecrated by God
Sovereign Lord / Master; the absolute owner and ruler who has authority over life and death
Jesus; from Hebrew Yeshua, meaning 'Yahweh saves'; the personal name of the incarnate Son of God
King; Jesus as the divinely appointed ruler over all kingdoms and peoples
The Beginning; the source and origin of all creation; the first cause of everything
Son of God; the eternal, divine Son in unique relationship with the Father
Son of Man; Jesus's most common self-designation, linking His humanity with the Danielic heavenly figure
I AM; Jesus's claim to the divine name of Exodus 3:14, asserting His eternal deity
The Resurrection; Jesus as the source and guarantee of resurrection life over death
The Great Shepherd; Jesus brought back from the dead as the shepherd who tends His flock eternally
Messiah / Anointed One; the promised deliverer who would be anointed with the Spirit of God
The Branch; the Messianic title for the righteous shoot of David who will reign as king
Root of Jesse; the Messiah who springs from David's lineage as a banner for all nations
Son of David; the Messianic heir to David's eternal throne and covenant
Prince of Peace; the Messianic name for the one who establishes everlasting peace
Wonderful Counselor; the Messianic ruler whose counsel is supernatural and beyond human wisdom
Everlasting Father; the Messianic title expressing His eternal care and fatherly provision
Servant of the LORD; the suffering servant who bears the sin of many
Lion of Judah; the triumphant Messiah who overcomes through sacrifice
High Priest; the Messianic mediator who makes atonement and intercedes for the people
Son of Man (Aramaic); the heavenly figure in Daniel given dominion over all peoples and nations
Root and Offspring of David; the Messiah who is both ancestor and descendant of David
Bright Morning Star; the Messiah as the harbinger of a new dawn who outshines all darkness
Prince / Ruler; the divinely appointed leader and ruler over Israel
The Creator of all things; the one who brought the universe into existence ex nihilo
The Eternal Judge; the divine figure of immeasurable age and glory who renders ultimate judgment
The righteous judge who will render perfect justice to all peoples everywhere

nameEn: God of Abraham, Isaac and Jacob
meaning: The covenant-keeping God who identifies Himself by relationship with the patriarchs
The Holy Spirit; the third person of the Trinity who sanctifies, comforts, and empowers
The one who rescues and delivers from enemies, danger, and bondage
A safe haven and shelter; God as the one to whom we flee in times of trouble
An impenetrable place of safety; the name of the LORD as protection for the righteous
An impregnable stronghold; God as the one who cannot be breached by any enemy
Unchanging foundation; God as the immovable rock on whom faith can be safely built
Divine protection; God who deflects attack and guards those who trust in Him
The compassionate shepherd who knows each sheep by name, leads, feeds, and lays down His life
The spiritual sustenance given by Christ; whoever comes to Him will never hunger
The life-giving presence of God; whoever drinks will never thirst again
The source of all spiritual fruitfulness; abiding in Him produces life and bearing of fruit
The Gate; the only way of access to salvation and to the Father
The divine light who illuminates all darkness; those who follow Him do not walk in darkness

nameEn: The Way, the Truth, and the Life
nameOriginal: ἡ ὁδὸς καὶ ἡ ἀλήθεια καὶ ἡ ζωή
transliteration: He Hodos kai he Aletheia kai he Zoe
meaning: Jesus as the path to God, the truth about God, and the source of eternal life
The supreme ruler over all earthly kings; no authority exists apart from His rule
Supreme authority over all masters; the one before whom every knee will bow

nameOriginal: ὁ ἀστὴρ ὁ λαμπρὸς ὁ πρωϊνός
transliteration: Ho Aster Ho Lampros Ho Proinos
meaning: The herald of a new eternal day; the radiant star who signals the coming dawn of God's kingdom
The first and last letters of the Greek alphabet; God encompasses all of existence from beginning to end
The eternal God who precedes and outlasts all of creation; there is none before or after Him
The triumphant, conquering Messiah from the tribe of Judah; worthy to open the sealed scroll
The perfect sacrificial lamb who takes away the sin of the world
The unique eternal Son who shares the divine nature of the Father
God with Us; the Messianic promise of God's intimate presence with His people
The one who establishes and rules in perfect shalom — wholeness, harmony, and reconciliation
The one whose counsel is miraculous and divine wisdom in all things
The eternal one who embodies fatherly love and care toward His people forever
The self-existent, self-defining God; eternal being who owes His existence to none other
The one called alongside to help; the Holy Spirit who comforts, guides, and advocates
The Father who consoles in every affliction and equips believers to comfort others
The God whose very nature is peace and who bestows peace that surpasses understanding
The source of all hope; the God who fills believers with joy and peace through trusting in Him
The God whose essential nature is agape love, expressed supremely in giving His Son
The God of all grace who calls believers to eternal glory through Christ
The majestic God whose glory fills all creation and is the ultimate end of all things
The perfectly holy God in covenant with Israel; His holiness both judges and saves
The one who pays the redemption price to restore what was lost through sin
The Father who is in heaven; the personal, relational God who provides and cares for His children
The divine rescuer who saves from sin, judgment, and eternal death
Jesus Christ as our legal advocate before the Father; the one who pleads our case
Jesus who holds authority over death; belief in Him guarantees eternal life
The supreme ruler who holds all authority; used especially in the prophets to emphasize divine power
God as Father; the most intimate relational title revealing His parental love and care
Spirit of God; the divine breath and presence of God moving over creation and inspiring His people
A mighty, powerful savior; the horn symbolizing strength in the animal world applied to God's saving power
The Messianic figure who rises with healing in His wings for those who fear God
The divine communication and revelation of God; in Christ, God's ultimate self-expression
King of the Universe/Eternity; used in Jewish blessings to acknowledge God's cosmic sovereignty
The First and the Last; the eternal God who precedes and outlasts all of history
God Who Forgives; the God who lifts and carries away transgression, iniquity, and sin
God who shepherds His people Israel; leads, protects, and gathers the flock
God's holy presence as an all-consuming fire that devours evil and purifies His people
